packageRules.description: PR-only, always (org policy): NOTHING auto-merges to main. The global minimumReleaseAge (7 days) plus minimumReleaseAgeBehaviour=timestamp-required hold every update until the release is a week old and timestamp-verified, blocking fast-moving supply-chain attacks like axios (Mar 2026), LiteLLM, xinference, TanStack/Mini Shai-Hulud. A human reviews and merges every PR. See https://blog.yossarian.net/2025/11/21/We-should-all-be-using-dependency-cooldowns and https://cooldowns.dev/
Security/vulnerability fixes bypass the COOLDOWN (PR raised immediately, not after 7 days) but are still PR-only - a human merges the CVE patch now.

Same-org HyperI packages bypass the cooldown - we publish these ourselves, supply-chain risk for our own packages is governed by our own CI gates and release process, not by external-attacker cooldown logic. Matches anything whose source URL points back to github.com/hyperi-io/* - crates.io, PyPI, npm, GitHub Actions, plus any GHCR images that set the org.opencontainers.image.source label (which the hyperi-ci container handler does by default).
Same-org HyperI container images bypass the cooldown - backstop for any ghcr.io/hyperi-io/* image that didn't get the source label set (which would otherwise miss the matchSourceUrls rule above).
Group all GitHub Actions updates (PR-only, like everything else)
